summary: Updated the vendored m2c tool to commit 581031e. The PR body explicitly notes improved support for `.L` style labels, and the diff shows broad parser/pattern-matcher changes around preserving dotted labels, distinguishing global/local/jump-target labels, merging consecutive labels, and matching label definitions/uses. It also refreshed m2c tooling dependencies and tests, moved CI to Python 3.8, updated Black pre-commit configuration, added `--knr` formatting documentation/CLI support, and expanded end-to-end coverage including gcc-division and mwcc27 outputs.
what_changed: Bumped the vendored `tools/m2c` snapshot to commit `581031e`.
Added support, at least in some cases, for `.L` style labels as stated in the PR text.
Changed `Label` in `asm_file.py` from a single `name` to `names: List[str]`, with comments explaining that consecutive labels can be merged so pattern code can keep assuming non-consecutive labels.
Stopped stripping leading dots from some labels and data symbol mentions; branch targets and local labels now preserve names such as `.L00400834` in more places.
Introduced `LabelKind` values for global, local, and jump-target label handling, and made `jlabel` explicitly process jump targets rather than treating it like `glabel`/`dlabel`.
Adjusted assembly argument parsing so bare branch-like names become `AsmGlobalSymbol("." + word)` rather than a separate `JumpTarget` argument in the parser path shown by the diff.
Extended pattern matching to track label definitions and label uses separately, allowing a symbolic label use to match any name in a merged label definition.
Added `AsmMatcher.branch_target_exists()` and used it in PPC branch replacement logic so a branch to an existing local target is not treated as an external/nonlocal branch replacement.
Updated MIPS sqrt patterns by splitting the previous GCC sqrt matcher into `GccSqrtPatternJal` and `GccSqrtPatternJalr`, including optional nop handling and a jalr-through-loaded-address variant.

Changed architecture `base_return_regs` from plain register lists to `(Register, floating)` tuples for MIPS and PPC, and updated translation/type logic to carry whether the guessed return register is floating point.
Added CLI/docs support for `--knr`, making it mutually exclusive with `--allman`; `--knr` sets function opening braces on separate lines without enabling full Allman behavior for if/else.
Updated Python tooling: CI setup from Python 3.7 to 3.8, Black hook source/revision to `black-pre-commit-mirror` 23.12.1, and Poetry metadata/lockfile to Python `^3.8`.
Regenerated or updated many m2c end-to-end expected outputs, with especially large churn in `tests/end_to_end/gcc-division`, including a new `mwcc27-o4p` assembly/output pair and flags files.
smart_moves: Preserving dotted label names instead of repeatedly stripping `.` avoids losing information needed to match `.L` style branch targets and jump table entries.
Representing a label as multiple names is a practical compatibility layer: it supports consecutive labels while minimizing disruption to existing pattern code that assumed a single label position.

Separating label definitions from label uses in pattern state is more robust than a single symbolic-label map because a merged label definition can legally satisfy multiple concrete branch target spellings.
Adding `branch_target_exists()` before PPC external-branch replacement prevents local branches from being accidentally rewritten as nonlocal control flow.
Encoding whether a base return register is floating point lets return-value inference and type propagation distinguish integer and floating return paths without hardcoding per-register checks later.
Updating the tests alongside the tool bump made the vendored tool update auditable despite the large generated-output diff.
decomp_lessons: When importing upstream decompiler tooling, capture the motivating parser behavior in the PR summary; here the key reusable fact is `.L` label support.

For assembly parsers, avoid normalizing away syntactic label prefixes unless every downstream user agrees on the normalized form; dotted local labels are semantically important for matching branch targets.
If pattern matchers assume no consecutive labels, either fix all assumptions or add an explicit merge representation like `Label.names` and teach lookup/matching code to search all aliases.
`jlabel` should be treated as an in-function jump target, not as a new global function label.
Branch-to-symbol heuristics should check whether the target exists locally before treating the branch as an external call/branch idiom.
Return register heuristics need type metadata; an integer register and a floating register may both be plausible base returns but imply different expression/type reconstruction.
Tool updates with large expected-output changes should include test fixtures that demonstrate the new syntax or compiler cases, such as `.L` labels and new mwcc27 gcc-division coverage.
naming_conventions: The bumped m2c recognizes local label prefixes/patterns including `loc_`, `locret_`, `def_`, `lbl_`, `LAB_`, and newly shown `jump_` in the local-label regex.
Dotted local labels such as `.L00400834` are preserved in labels and branch targets instead of being stored as `L00400834`.
MIPS sqrt patterns were named specifically by call form: `GccSqrtPatternJal` and `GccSqrtPatternJalr`.
The new brace-style option is named `--knr` and is documented as `K&R braces`; it is mutually exclusive with `--allman`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `Label.names` when searching blocks by label; the diff updates flow-graph lookup from `block.label.name == label` to membership in `block.label.names`.
Keep branch target strings in the same dotted form in both definitions and uses so `.L...` targets match directly.
For pattern definitions ending in `:`, construct `Label([part[:-1]])` rather than a single-name label.
For symbolic label matching, record the label definition once and allow uses only if they are contained in the definition's merged names.
Add optional instruction slots such as `nop?` in patterns when compiler output may include or omit delay-slot nops.
Match both direct `jal sqrtf` and indirect `lui/addiu/jalr` sqrtf call sequences for GCC MIPS sqrt lowering.
For PPC store-update instructions, update the base register and then store memory with separate handling for register-offset and address-mode forms, as shown by the arch_ppc eval changes.
Treat `gp_rel` like `sda2`/`sda21` in address-mode macro offset extraction, according to the translate.py diff excerpt.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rely on `.L` label parsing in tools/m2c.
Search for follow-up changes to `Label.names` or consecutive label handling.
Search for later m2c updates touching `base_return_regs` and return type inference.
Search for regressions or fixes around `jlabel` parsing and jump table labels.
Search for decomp PRs that mention using `--knr` m2c formatting.
